channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Conflicts on rise globally, highest level since WWII, data shows
item.description: Global conflicts surged to the highest number tallied by Sweden's Uppsala security research group.
item.content:encoded:
Global conflicts surged to the highest number tallied by Sweden's Uppsala security research group.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools
item.description: Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.
item.content:encoded:
Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.

(Image credit: James Brooks)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says pilots are fine after U.S. helicopter crashes near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A U.S. Army Apache attack helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, with President Trump saying the two crew members on board were "fine" after the incident. Trump also expressed optimism over negotiations with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. Army Apache attack helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, with President Trump saying the two crew members on board were "fine" after the incident. Trump also expressed optimism over negotiations with Iran.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pentagon labels tech giant Alibaba and car maker BYD as aiding Chinese military
item.description: The Pentagon has added several prominent Chinese businesses to its list of Chinese military companies. The move prevents them from securing U.S. defense contracts.
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon has added several prominent Chinese businesses to its list of Chinese military companies. The move prevents them from securing U.S. defense contracts.

(Image credit: Mahesh Kumar A.)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas
item.description: A federal judge on Monday struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The administration announced the fee as a way of preventing foreign workers from taking American jobs.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge on Monday struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The administration announced the fee as a way of preventing foreign workers from taking American jobs.

(Image credit: Wilfredo Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz's 3-month closure could set a dangerous precedent, experts worry
item.description: Has the closure of the Strait of Hormuz set a new — and dangerous — precedent for international shipping lanes?
item.content:encoded: Has the closure of the Strait of Hormuz set a new — and dangerous — precedent for international shipping lanes?
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
item.description: More Americans are rethinking where they want to live. Some are heading to Southeast Asia, drawn in part by what they're seeing on TikTok and YouTube. But those videos don't tell the whole story.
item.content:encoded: More Americans are rethinking where they want to live. Some are heading to Southeast Asia, drawn in part by what they're seeing on TikTok and YouTube. But those videos don't tell the whole story.
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: Xi and Kim express hopes for greater ties between China and North Korea
item.description: Xi traveled to Pyongyang on Monday in a likely attempt to reassert China's unique influence over its socialist neighbor.
item.content:encoded:
Xi traveled to Pyongyang on Monday in a likely attempt to reassert China's unique influence over its socialist neighbor.

(Image credit: 朝鮮通信社)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel-Iran strikes threaten truce. And, Ebola is spreading at an unprecedented rate
item.description: Israel and Iran's recent exchange of fire is threatening the truce in the Middle East. And, the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is spreading at an unprecedented rate, officials say.
item.content:encoded:
Israel and Iran's recent exchange of fire is threatening the truce in the Middle East. And, the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is spreading at an unprecedented rate, officials say.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: In speech to Spanish parliament, pope demands respect for the dignity of all people
item.description: In the first papal address to the Spanish legislature, the American pope said a "moral renewal" was necessary in legislatures and public life to ensure respect for the inherent dignity of all people.
item.content:encoded:
In the first papal address to the Spanish legislature, the American pope said a "moral renewal" was necessary in legislatures and public life to ensure respect for the inherent dignity of all people.

(Image credit: Yara Nardi/Reuters Pool via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
